about.heroSubtitle: A powerful voice dedicated to the protection of human rights.
about.history: RMAS was established in 2020 with registration number 4120/2020, aiming to provide a platform for the oppressed and marginalized communities.
about.mission: Our mission is not just to register complaints, but to ensure that the backward and oppressed get their rights.
about.vision: A Bharat where there is no police atrocity, corruption, or discrimination.

unity.desc: Serving humanity above caste and religion.

card1.name: Saroj Sekha Sengale
card1.short: Delay in justice is equal to denial of justice. Our organization is the strength of those whose voices have been suppressed and their rights violated.
card1.full: Delay in justice is equal to denial of justice. Our organization is the strength of those whose voices have been suppressed and their rights violated. National Human Rights Organization (RMAS) was founded on the belief that even the most marginalized person in India should know they are not alone. As National President, my aim is not merely to enforce rules, but to build a society where justice is an inherent right, not a privilege. Over the years, we have stood with victims from Kashmir to Kanyakumari to deliver justice, and this is a testament to the unwavering courage of our workers. Today, as the world moves toward technology, our organization is also modernizing. I am proud that under the leadership of Bihar State President Shri Bhola Prasad Mahato ji, the entire team has made technology a tool of service. This digital portal created by Bihar I.T. Cell is part of our mission, so that justice is no longer confined to office files, but reaches every victim through technology. I salute the visionary spirit and hard work of the Bihar team. Let us all come together to build a fearless and just India. As long as one person in society is unsafe, our work is incomplete. Truth may be troubled, but it cannot be defeated. We will fight and win—for humanity, for your rights.

card2.name: Shri Bhola Prasad Mahato
card2.short: Our identity, our pride: a pledge for a just Bihar.
card2.full: Our identity, our pride: a pledge for a just Bihar. Human rights are not a gift given by anyone; they are the respect and rights every human being receives by birth. Our goal is to empower the people of Bihar with awareness and strength so they never feel helpless. RMAS is not just a name, but hope for victims and a loud voice against injustice. The true power of the organization lies in its dedicated workers and leaders. I am proud of our Bihar team, where colleagues like Shri Deepak ji, Mohammad Rafiq ji and Raju ji bring intellectual strength, while Shambhu Sharan ji, Avinash ji and Rambilas ji inspire us with grassroots struggle. Rinki Kumari ji and Rajesh ji represent women’s power and youth energy, and our father figure Baleshwar ji and Shiv Shankar ji are our greatest assets. Murad ji, Kunal ji and media-incharge Md. Jawed Akhter have brought revolutionary change in technology. I salute the spirit of these warriors. In this changing era, I am proud that our Bihar team has made technology our weapon. The digital portal developed by our I.T. Cell is a major step toward transparency and accountability. Our services and membership are now reaching the people directly, which proves a corruption-free system. I urge all district, block and panchayat leaders to treat their position not as prestige, but as a means of service. Go out into the field, wipe the tears of victims, and stand at the forefront of their struggle. With your cooperation, we will become the voice of every oppressed person in Bihar. You are my pride, and your hard work is our greatest achievement. As long as injustice exists, our struggle continues. Let us build a Bihar where humanity is supreme.

card3.name: Shri Deepak Kumar
card3.role: Chairman of the Advisory Committee, Bihar
card3.alt: Chairman of the Advisory Committee Bihar
card3.short: Human rights are not just words in law books; they are the last hope of every person who suffers injustice, exploitation and neglect.
card3.full: Human rights are not just words in law books; they are the last hope of every person who suffers injustice, exploitation and neglect. I began my journey in 2021 as a district organization secretary and served as district president (Katihar) and divisional president (Purnia). Today, as the chairman of the advisory committee, this responsibility is not just a position but a test of truth, service and dedication. This responsibility has been entrusted to me by respected Bhola Prasad Mahato ji, whose guidance and trust inspire me to serve with the utmost sincerity. In human rights work, I have learned that every victim needs trust before justice — trust that someone will listen and stand with the truth. I urge all our officials and volunteers to see their role as responsibility, not authority. A position does not raise us; our conduct gives it dignity. The greatest strength of this organization is its committed worker who stands with the victim selflessly. We must always remember that the path of justice is difficult, but it is the most sacred path. Today we must make the organization stronger, transparent and sensitive, connect youth and women to the human rights movement, and bring the light of justice to the most marginalized. If we all work with honesty, discipline and service, RMAS will become the true voice of humanity. Let us promise together that there will be no compromise in protecting human dignity. That is our duty and our real service to the nation.

card4.name: Md. Jawed Akhter
card4.alt: I.T. Cell and Media Incharge Bihar
card4.short: My journey began humbly in 2022 when I joined RMAS as the Falka Block Media Incharge.
card4.full: My journey began humbly in 2022 when I joined RMAS as the Falka Block Media Incharge. Under the guidance of Shri Deepak Kumar sir and Mohammad Rafiq Alam ji, I learned the real nuances of human rights, the pain of victims, and the struggle for justice. Because of my dedication, within three months I was entrusted with the responsibility of Katihar district media incharge. The journey did not stop there. Seeing my hard work and loyalty, the state leadership gave me the larger responsibility of Bihar state media incharge. Honestly, I felt both hesitation and fear. The larger the role, the heavier the responsibility. I asked myself — am I worthy of this position? Can I handle this duty? But respected State President Shri Bhola Prasad Mahato ji recognized the courage within me. His encouragement, trust and motivation gave me new energy. He made me realize that “true service begins beyond fear”. “When courage meets determination, even difficulties start showing the way.” During our work I clearly felt that in today’s digital age RMAS needs a modern, transparent and strong website. I resolved to build that website myself, and at the same time I was given the additional responsibility of I.T. Cell Incharge. Today I am serving both Media and I.T. Cell with complete dedication, hard work and commitment. “If you want to light the lamp of service, you must also sacrifice your nights. Loyalty is the fire that keeps the path of justice bright even in darkness.” My goal is not just to build technology systems, but to amplify every victim’s voice, share their story widely, and accelerate the fight for justice. I firmly believe that with the support of all of you and the blessings of state leadership, we will make RMAS an example and a strong institution not only in Bihar, but across the country. “When determination is strong, success comes by itself. When dedication is true, humanity’s voice can never be silenced.”
